comment: Both ontologies define a class labeled Person with the same intended meaning.
Exact lexical match on Document; both represent documents.
Exact lexical match on Review; both are document-like review artifacts.
Exact lexical match on Session; both denote conference sessions.
Exact lexical match on Tutorial; both model tutorial events/lectures.
EKAW Presenter and IASTED Speaker both denote persons giving presentations, but labels and axioms are not fully identical.
Near-exact lexical match differing only by underscore/capitalization; both denote the chair of a session.
EKAW Student is broader than IASTED Student_lecturer, which is specifically a student who is also a lecturer.
A possible reviewer is broader than an actual reviewer; IASTED Reviewer is someone who gives a review.
Both classes denote authors of submitted/presented papers or manuscripts in the conference domain.
Conference_Participant and Delegate both represent a person attending or participating in the conference.
Program committee member in EKAW is close to technical committee member in IASTED, though committee naming conventions differ.
Both are document artifacts related to scholarly submission, but an abstract is not equivalent to a submission.
Camera-ready paper corresponds closely to final manuscript, both being the post-acceptance final version.
Submitted_Paper is close to Initial_manuscipt as both represent the initial version sent for review.
Accepted paper is related to final manuscript through the publication workflow, but EKAW explicitly distinguishes accepted from camera-ready.
Rejected_Paper denotes a rejected artifact, while Refusing_manuscript appears to denote the rejection process/activity rather than the paper itself.
Conference banquet and dinner banquet refer to the same kind of social event in the conference context.
Both denote an excursion/trip associated with the conference, though duration/detail may differ.
Both classes cover social activities organized for conference participants.
EKAW Location and IASTED Place both represent locations where conference-related entities occur.
University is broader than Presenter_university, which seems to denote a university associated with a presenter.
Proceedings are a specific kind of publication; IASTED Publication is broader.
Conference proceedings closely match book proceedings as a proceedings publication format.
Workshop has no clear direct equivalent in IASTED; Session is only loosely related as a conference activity.
Both properties relate a document/item to its author/writer; one is inverse-style naming with spelling variation.
EKAW authorOf links person to document, while IASTED write links person to item; they are closely related but not strictly equivalent.
A reviewer relation in EKAW is related to the writer of a review in IASTED, but the properties connect different argument structures.
EKAW hasReview is a property, while IASTED Review is a class; included as a weak related mapping only because no direct property match is present.
Both properties express where an event/activity takes place, though domain/range differ somewhat between ontologies.
